gaps.why_it_matters: The trim branch's interaction of slug[:max_len] with rstrip('-') at exactly the boundary is the documented no-trailing-dash invariant, and no test pins it (the existing test only asserts <= 40 on one input)
gaps.suggested_test: inputs crafted so the cut lands on a dash: assert result length and no trailing '-' for max_len in {1, 5, 40}

gaps.why_it_matters: The regex only preserves ascii a-z0-9, so unicode letters vanish into dashes ('café' -> 'caf') — surprising behavior with zero coverage
gaps.suggested_test: assert slugify('café ümlaut') documents the ascii-only contract

gaps.why_it_matters: slugify('') and all-punctuation inputs return '' — untested
